methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: Vault must hold sufficient liquid WRON to pay accumulated operator fees
invariants.attack_scenario: If rewards are restaked via harvestAndDelegateRewards, operatorFeeAmount increases but vault WRON balance does not. When fees are claimed, it drains WRON deposited by users, causing insolvency and preventing user withdrawals.

invariants.description: Total assets must exclude accumulated operator fees to prevent share price inflation
invariants.attack_scenario: Because totalAssets() includes operatorFeeAmount, the share price is artificially inflated. When the operator claims fees, totalAssets() drops, causing the share price to drop. Attackers can exploit this by redeeming shares before fees are claimed, stealing yield from other depositors.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_total_assets_excludes_fees() public view { uint256 expected = IERC20(asset()).balanceOf(address(this)) + getTotalStaked() + getTotalRewards() - operatorFeeAmount; assert(totalAssets() == expected); }

invariants.description: Escrow must hold sufficient WRON to cover all unredeemed assets from finalized withdrawal epochs
invariants.attack_scenario: If the vault fails to transfer enough WRON during finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch, the Escrow will be underfunded. Users attempting to redeem their finalized withdrawals will face reverts, locking their funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_escrow_fully_funded() public view { assert(IERC20(asset()).balanceOf(escrow) >= totalPendingRedemptions); }

invariants.description: Total shares locked in an epoch must equal the sum of shares requested by all users for that epoch
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user bypasses request accounting, they could inflate lockedSharesPerEpoch without burning their own shares, leading to an over-allocation of assets to the epoch and draining the vault.

invariants.description: Share price (assets per share) must be monotonically non-decreasing
invariants.attack_scenario: If the share price decreases due to fee accounting bugs or slashing, attackers can front-run the decrease by withdrawing, leaving remaining users to absorb the loss.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_share_price_monotonicity() public view { assert(totalAssets() * previousTotalSupply >= previousTotalAssets * totalSupply()); }

invariants.description: Total assets redeemed by users for a specific epoch must not exceed the total assets allocated to that epoch
invariants.attack_scenario: If rounding errors or manipulation allowed users to redeem more assets than allocated, the Escrow contract would be drained, leaving other users unable to redeem their shares.
